title: I Built an App in 58 Days With AI: What Actually Worked vs. The Hype
author: Alina Bezchotnikova
text:
Member-only story
I Built an App in 58 Days With AI: What Actually Worked vs. The Hype
So there I was in April, 3 AM, staring at my screen, trying to convince an AI to fix the same bug for the dozenth time just to read “I fixed the issue!” when nothing was clearly fixed.
When vibe-coding tools started to take off, it very quickly became my hobby. I tried a few ideas before landing on something I actually wanted to do. “I want to help people to be better with their finances!” was my main slogan. I’d spent over a month and about 27 attempts to rebuild it from scratch. And you know what I had to show after?
Nothing. The app didn’t work. And at that point, I just felt done.
Today you can find the app on App Store and Google Play. But this is not a promo or an attempt to brag. This is an honest breakdown of how my journey looked like, how much it cost, and what it actually means for the software development industry.
So what the hell happened between April and October?
When the Magic Wore Off
To give you some context, I’m a UX designer with 17 years of experience and the last 10 years I’ve spent deeply in B2B SaaS. I can read code, I can understand what it’s supposed to do, but writing is not my thing.
